headline: Pharmacy-Led Services Limited Despite 14,000 Registered Pharmacists
context: Malaysia's pharmacists are underutilized for primary healthcare delivery despite capacity to provide expanded services.

cards.big: 14,000 registered pharmacists could deliver vaccinations and health screening but are not permitted
cards.sub: Scope of practice restrictions limit pharmacists to dispensing not clinical services

cards.big: Poisons Act 1952 restricts pharmacist prescribing and vaccination authority
cards.sub: 74-year-old legislation does not reflect modern pharmaceutical training and capability

cards.big: Average distance to nearest pharmacy is 1.2km versus 5.4km to nearest government clinic
cards.sub: Pharmacies are 4 times more accessible than clinics for community health delivery

cards.big: UK pharmacists administer 15 million vaccinations annually under expanded practice since 2015
cards.sub: Pharmacist immunization authority reduced GP workload by 12% and improved coverage

cards.big: 14,000 healthcare professionals restricted to dispensing while the system faces doctor shortages is wasteful
Should pharmacists be authorized to provide vaccinations and basic health screening services
